---
title: "AI vs. FME / geospatial tools?"
author:
  - name: Ralph Straumann
    url: https://ralphstraumann.ch
date: "2026-05-28 19:37"
image: "nam-anh-QJbyG6O0ick-unsplash 1.jpg"
description: "Safe Software and the #FME community discusses whether #AI #agents are replacing tools like #FME for geospatial #ETL work. The community thread and blog post are worth reading for what they imply about the future of #low-code geospatial tools more broadly."
---

Via [Stephan Heuel][toot][^fme-vs-code] I came across this discussion in the 
FME[^fme] ecosystem: [*The Elephant in the Room?*][discussion] The elephant, of 
course, is AI and AI agents and their rising role in conceiving, implementing, 
and executing geospatial data pipelines. A short excerpt from the thread:

> My default tool for random tasks or jobs was FME, now its Claude Code/Cowork. 
Using FME just feels way too slow now by comparison. Microsoft Copilot is even 
in Excel now, I never thought I'd go back to using Excel!?!? (...) 
> 
> Everytime I open up FME now I feel so unproductive because of the time it 
takes to get something done. It feels painfully slow compared to having the 
Agent just "Do it". 

Safe Software^[Maker of FME.] picked the discussion in the Safe Software 
Community Hub up in a post of their own: [*Are AI Coding Agents Replacing Tools 
Like FME? Let’s Talk About It*][post]. 

[Betteridge's law of headlines][betteridge] applies: "Any headline that ends in 
a question mark can be answered by the word *No*." Still: I agree with 
[Stephan][toot]'s kudos towards Safe Software for their openness to discuss the 
topic. 

Both the [original discussion thread][discussion] and the [Safe Software 
post][post] are worth reading. The opinions expressed do not only matter for 
FME, I would say, but more broadly for similarly positioned tools and solutions. 


[toot]: https://swiss.social/@ping13/116554171212345300
[discussion]: https://community.safe.com/fme-special-interest-group-ai-93/the-elephant-in-the-room-40309
[post]: https://fme.safe.com/blog/2026/05/are-ai-coding-agents-replacing-tools-like-fme-lets-talk-about-it/
[betteridge]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betteridge's_law_of_headlines

[^fme]: Safe Software's Feature Manipulation Engine.

[^fme-vs-code]: Side-track: 15 years ago I worked with Stephan, but my take on 
FME was *very* different from what he expresses in his post: While I appreciated 
its robustness compared to other low-code tools for geospatial workflows 
(especially Model Builder at the time), I did not like FME overall and preferred 
to write code instead. Granted: That (a small) part of my role included 
maintaining, repairing, and running fickle FME workspaces that had been built 
by someone else could have something to do with that 🙂.
